---
title: "Module Schedule"
page-layout: full
---

## Course Modules (7.5 Weeks)

The schedule below maps each module to its slides, activity loop, and assessment. All seven modules' slide decks are published; checkpoint dates and project deadlines are marked TODO pending final calendar confirmation.

| Week | Topic | Slides | Activity Loop | Readings | Assessment |
|------|-------|--------|--------------|----------|------------|
| 1 | **Linear Models & Least Squares** | [Module 1]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m1-linear-least-squares/) | [week01-least-squares]({{< var activities_url >}}/week01-least-squares/) | [PML Ch. 1 (§1.1–1.3)](readings/PML_Wk1_Ch1_Intro_s1.1-1.3.pdf) & [§1.5 (data)](readings/PML_Ch1_s1.5_Data_pp49-57.pdf); [MML](https://mml-book.github.io/) Ch. 2 & Ch. 9 (§9.1–9.2) | — |
| 2 | **Generalization** | [Module 2]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m2-generalization/) | [week02-bias-variance]({{< var activities_url >}}/week02-bias-variance/) | [PML §4.7 (bias–variance)](readings/PML_Wk2_Ch4_MLE_and_Frequentist_s4.1-4.2_s4.7.pdf) & [§11.3 (ridge)](https://probml.github.io/pml-book/book1.html); [MML](https://mml-book.github.io/) Ch. 5 (review) | Checkpoint 1 — normal equations (TODO) |
| 3 | **MLE & Parameter Uncertainty** | [Module 3]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m3-mle-uncertainty/) | — | [PML Ch. 2–3](readings/PML_Wk3_Probability_Ch2_Ch3_plus_Ch4_Bayes.pdf); [§4.1–4.2 (MLE, incl. §4.2.5) & §4.7 (Fisher info)](readings/PML_Wk2_Ch4_MLE_and_Frequentist_s4.1-4.2_s4.7.pdf); [MML](https://mml-book.github.io/) Ch. 6 (review) | Checkpoint 2 — MLE (TODO) |
| 4 | **Bayesian Inference** | [Module 4]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m4-bayesian-inference/) | [week03-bayesian-updating]({{< var activities_url >}}/week03-bayesian-updating/) | [PML §4.6 (Bayes & conjugacy)](readings/PML_Wk3_Probability_Ch2_Ch3_plus_Ch4_Bayes.pdf) & [§11.7 (Bayesian linear regression)](https://probml.github.io/pml-book/book1.html); [Think Bayes Ch. 2](https://allendowney.github.io/ThinkBayes2/chap02.html) & [Ch. 4](https://allendowney.github.io/ThinkBayes2/chap04.html) (supp.: [Ch. 18](https://allendowney.github.io/ThinkBayes2/chap18.html)) | Checkpoint 3 — posterior (TODO); **Project 1 due (TODO)** |
| 5 | **Approximate Inference & Bayesian Classification** | [Module 5]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m5-approximate-inference/) | [week04-logistic-regression]({{< var activities_url >}}/week04-logistic-regression/) | [PML §10.1–10.2 (logistic; review Ch. 9)](readings/PML_Wk4_Ch10_Logistic_Regression_s10.1-10.2.pdf) & [§4.6.8 (Laplace)](readings/PML_Wk3_Probability_Ch2_Ch3_plus_Ch4_Bayes.pdf); [Think Bayes Ch. 19 (MCMC)](https://allendowney.github.io/ThinkBayes2/chap19.html); optional: [MML](https://mml-book.github.io/) Ch. 7 (Newton/IRLS) | Checkpoint 4 — Newton–Raphson (TODO); **Project 2 assigned** |
| 6 | **SVM & Clustering** | [Module 6]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m6-svm-clustering/) | [week05-kmeans]({{< var activities_url >}}/week05-kmeans/) | [PML §21.3–21.4 (k-means, GMM/EM)](readings/PML_Wk5_Ch21_KMeans_and_Mixtures_s21.3-21.4.pdf); [MML](https://mml-book.github.io/) Ch. 11 — SVM lecture-only (optional pointer: [PML §17.3](https://probml.github.io/pml-book/book1.html)) | Checkpoint 5 — k-means/PCA (TODO) |
| 7 | **PCA** | [Module 7]({{< var slides_url >}}/modules/m7-pca/) | [week06-pca]({{< var activities_url >}}/week06-pca/) | [MML](https://mml-book.github.io/) §4.2 (eigendecomposition) & Ch. 10 (PCA); [PML §20.1 (secondary)](readings/PML_Wk6_Ch20_PCA_s20.1.pdf) | Checkpoint retakes conclude; **Project 2 due (TODO)** |

*(Activity slugs are week-named and land one week after their module opens from Module 4 on — modules ≠ weeks. Module 3 has no peer loop — its [parameter-uncertainty demo](https://gchism94.github.io/info521-activities-2026/m3-parameter-uncertainty/) is used in lecture, not as a loop. Cross-validation (M2) is lecture-only — no assigned reading.)*

**Where next (optional — not a module, no assessment):** Neural networks ([UDL Ch. 2–3](readings/UDL_Wk7_Ch2-3_Neural-Networks_pp31-54.pdf), [Ch. 6–7](readings/UDL_Wk7_Ch6-7_Training-Backprop_pp91-131.pdf)) continue in INFO 557.

---

## Legend

- **Slides**: Links to module landing pages listing the live reveal.js decks (dark/light toggle, top-right; a rendered `.pdf` ships alongside each deck).
- **Activity Loop**: Interactive tool + retakeable mastery quiz → reflection → peer review → revise (completion tokens honor-verified).
- **Readings**: all open-licensed — **PML** = Murphy, *Probabilistic Machine Learning: An Introduction* (2022); **MML** = Deisenroth, Faisal & Ong, *Mathematics for Machine Learning*; **Think Bayes** = Downey, *Think Bayes* (2e); **UDL** = Prince, *Understanding Deep Learning*. PML/UDL excerpt links are hosted with this site; MML, Think Bayes, and un-excerpted PML sections (§11.3, §11.7, §17.3) link to the books' free official pages. Section numbers follow PML1 (2022) — verify against your printing.
- **Assessment**: Checkpoint sittings and project deadlines (calendar TBD).

All external links open in a new tab.
